frameworks.name: temporal difference reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: Unsigned components of TD error mapped onto dendritic branch responses

frameworks.name: dendritic gain modulation
frameworks.one_line: Apical dendrites gate recruitment of task-relevant neurons via salience

frameworks.name: associative learning in neocortex
frameworks.one_line: Pyramidal dendrites as sites for sensory-outcome association

core_question: How do L5 pyramidal apical dendrites adapt during goal-directed sensory learning?
key_assumptions: Calcium signals index apical dendritic activity
Unsigned TD-error components are biologically separable
Branch-level responses reflect functional task variables
manipulations: Texture discrimination go/no-go learning across naive to expert stages
Optogenetic apical inhibition during outcome time window
Reinforcement learning model perturbations matched to experimental perturbations
key_findings: Diverse task-related dendritic responses, branch-localized or tuft-wide
Tuft-level go/no-go discrimination remains poor even in expert mice
Reward-outcome responses decrease as task proficiency grows
Outcome-predicting touch responses strengthen with learning
interpretations: Branch response types match distinct unsigned TD-error components
Apical salience signals facilitate recruitment of task-relevant neurons via dendritic gain
limitations_noted: Tuft-level discrimination of go/no-go stimuli remains poor
